Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Maithili (code=mai, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Bihar
- district: Purnia
- first_language: Maithili
- clinical_persona_summary: Male, 18, never married, retired, primary education, rural, state: Bihar, district: Purnia, first language: Maithili. Agrarian lifestyle with seasonal farming duties, limited health check-up adherence, and savings under the mattress. Fluent in spoken Hindi, reads simple texts, and follows cricket and kabaddi.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
डिस्चार्ज समरी — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Purnia]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ramesh Kumar]] जन्म तिथि [[DOB|2006-04-15]] [[AGE|18]] [[GENDER|Male]]
भर्ती तिथि [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0815-001]] वार्ड [[WARD_NUMBER|A1]] बेड [[BED_NUMBER|05]]
डॉक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sanjay Prasad]] | उपचार / सलाह: रोगी 18 वर्षीय पुरुष छथि, जिनका ग्रामीण पृष्ठभूमि सँ छथि आ पूर्णिया जिला, बिहार सँ छथि। ओ तीन दिन सँ तेज बुखार, कंपकंपी आ शरीर मे दर्दक शिकायत कऽ रहल छलाह। परीक्षणक समय हुनका तेज बुखार (102.4°F), तीव्र हृदय गति (110 bpm) आ हल्का निर्जलीकरण (dehydration) छल। हुनकर रक्तचाप (blood pressure) 110/70 mmHg छल। जाँच सँ पता चलल जे हुनका मे ल्यूकोसाइटोसिस (leukocytosis) आ न्यूट्रोफिलिया (neutrophilia) अछि आ C-रिएक्टिव प्रोटीन (C-reactive protein) बढ़ल अछि। ब्लड कल्चर कयल गेल आ रैपिड मलेरिया एंटीजन टेस्ट नकारात्मक आयल। हुनका पुनर्जलीकरण (rehydration) लेल इंट्रावेनस फ्लूइड्स आ बुखार लेल सेफ्ट्रिएक्सोन (ceftriaxone) आ पैरासिटामोलक अनुभवजन्य उपचार शुरू कयल गेल। भर्तीक दोसर दिन, हुनकर ब्लड कल्चर मे साल्मोनेला टाइफी (Salmonella Typhi) भेटल, जे टाइफाइड बुखारक पुष्टि करैत अछि। एंटीबायोटिक रेजिमेन केँ ओरल एजिथ्रोमाइसिन (oral azithromycin) मे बदलि देल गेल। अगिला 48 घंटा मे रोगी मे महत्वपूर्ण नैदानिक सुधार देखल गेल, बुखार उतरि गेल आ भूख बढ़ि गेल। डिस्चार्ज सँ पहिने ओ 24 घंटा धरि बुखार मुक्त आ नैदानिक रूप सँ स्थिर रहलाह। हुनका पूरा 7 दिनक एजिथ्रोमाइसिन कोर्स पूरा करबाक, पर्याप्त हाइड्रेशन रखबाक आ एक सप्ताह मे आउटपेशेंट डिपार्टमेंट मे फॉलो-अप करबाक सलाह देल गेल। हुनका भविष्यक संक्रमण सँ बचबाक लेल भोजन आ पानि क स्वच्छता पर सेहो परामर्श देल गेल। रोगीक पिता, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sohan Kumar]], डिस्चार्ज काउंसलिंगक समय उपस्थित छलाह आ निर्देश केँ बुझबाक पुष्टि कयलनि। रोगीक ABHA ID [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] अछि आ हुनकर ABHA पता [[ABHA_ADDRESS|ramesh.kumar@abdm]] अछि। रोगी [[CASTE|SC]] समुदाय सँ छथि। फॉलो-अप लेल संपर्क नंबर [[PHONE_NUMBER|9431023456]] अछि। जिला [[DISTRICT|Purnia]] अछि।

judge_reasoning: Maithili Devanagari prose matches expectation; persona, clinical content and entities align; no script/dialect or length issues; Latin terms and tagged IDs permitted.

professional_persona: Jancy John, a 26‑year‑old homemaker with a flair for Kerala cuisine and meticulous budgeting, channels their love for structure into flawlessly organized church events and is now turning that disciplined energy into a fledgling home‑based catering venture.
linguistic_persona: Jancy John, fluent in Malayalam and equipped with functional English for reading notes, digital messaging, and basic online tasks, handles daily communication in their mother tongue while using English only when necessary, and has no formal training in Hindi.
sports_persona: Jancy John, who enjoys playing badminton at the local community centre and follows the Kerala Blasters in the Indian Super League, keeps fit through regular yoga sessions and weekend badminton matches, balancing competitive spirit with a relaxed approach to exercise.
arts_persona: Jancy John, an avid reader of Vaikom Muhammad Basheer and O.N.V. Kurup, delights in singing hymns with their church choir and unwinds by watching Mohanlal’s classic Malayalam films, weaving literary and cinematic influences into their everyday creativity.
travel_persona: Jancy John, who prefers well‑planned trips within a modest budget, often explores Kerala’s scenic spots such as Munnar, Alleppey’s backwaters, and the historic town of Fort Kochi, and dreams of a cultural pilgrimage to Delhi’s monuments and a short overseas getaway to Singapore, balancing family commitments with a love for gentle adventure.
culinary_persona: Jancy John, a skilled home chef specializing in traditional Kerala fare, excels at making fluffy appams, aromatic vegetable stew, and sweet payasam, frequently seasoning with fresh curry leaves, coconut milk, and tamarind, and delights in sourcing herbs from their kitchen garden to elevate family meals and church feasts.
persona: Jancy John, a 26‑year‑old meticulous homemaker who balances a love for structured budgeting with a secret obsession for spicy street snacks, turns everyday chores into mini‑projects and dreams of scaling their cherished Kerala recipes into a thriving catering venture.
cultural_background: Jancy John belongs to the Malayali Syrian Christian community in the rural outskirts of Thrissur, Kerala. Her family follows a blend of traditional Kerala customs—like Onam feasts, folk art, and agrarian rhythms—and Christian practices centered around the local parish, weekly Sunday worship, and communal celebrations of Christmas and Easter. The close‑knit village network provides a structured social fabric that aligns with her preference for predictability and organized routines.
linguistic_background: Jancy's first language is Malayalam, which she reads, writes and speaks fluently, handling household affairs, managing finances and communicating within her community. Her higher secondary education provided her with functional English; she can read simple texts, write basic notes, and understand spoken instructions, though she prefers Malayalam for most daily interactions. She has no formal training in Hindi or any other language.
skills_and_expertise: As a homemaker in a rural Kerala household, Jancy excels at managing day-to-day domestic operations. She is adept at cooking traditional Kerala cuisine, especially vegetarian fare for church feasts, and maintains meticulous household budgeting and expense tracking. Her organizational skills extend to coordinating church events and festivals, where she oversees logistics, menu planning, and volunteer scheduling. She possesses basic computer and smartphone proficiency for handling family finances, online shopping, and staying connected through messaging apps. Additionally, she tends to a modest kitchen garden, cultivating vegetables and herbs, reflecting her practical, hands‑on approach.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Malayalam household management', 'Kerala cuisine cooking', 'Budgeting and expense tracking', 'Church event coordination', 'Basic computer and smartphone usage', 'Gardening and small-scale farming', 'Volunteer management']
hobbies_and_interests: Jancy enjoys immersing herself in Malayalam literature, often reading novels by Vaikom Muhammad Basheer and poetry by O.N.V. Kurup. She finds relaxation in tending to her kitchen garden, growing vegetables like okra, ladyfinger and herbs such as curry leaves. Cooking traditional Kerala dishes—especially appams, stew, and payasam—for family and church events is a creative outlet for her. She participates in the church choir, singing hymns during services. In her leisure time, she watches Malayalam movies, plays badminton with friends, and practices basic yoga for health.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Reading Malayalam literature', 'Gardening', 'Cooking traditional Kerala dishes', 'Church choir singing', 'Watching Malayalam movies', 'Playing badminton', 'Practicing yoga']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Jancy aspires to turn her passion for cooking and community service into a sustainable home‑based catering venture, initially focusing on church gatherings, weddings, and cultural events in Thrissur. She plans to enroll in a certificate program in nutrition or culinary arts to professionalise her skills and enhance the healthfulness of her menus. In the longer term, she hopes to become a coordinator for the local parish’s women’s ministry, organizing skill‑building workshops and outreach programs that empower other homemakers. These goals reflect her desire for structured growth, practical impact, and a balanced contribution to family and community.
persona_clinical_summary: A 26-year-old female homemaker from Thrissur, Kerala, married and educated to higher secondary level, speaks Malayalam and functional English. She resides in a rural zone and follows a Malayali Syrian Christian cultural background with traditional customs. Clinically, she has a structured lifestyle with disciplined budgeting and a love for Kerala cuisine.

PHC രജിസ്റ്റർ — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Primary Health Centre, Chavakkad]] ഗ്രാമം [[VILLAGE|Kadungallur]] ജില്ല [[DISTRICT|Thrissur]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Annamma Thomas]] [[AGE|26]] [[GENDER|Female]] | സങ്കീർണ്ണമായ വിട്ടുമാറാത്ത പരിചരണത്തിനായുള്ള തുടർപരിശോധന

രോഗി 26 വയസ്സുള്ള ഒരു വീട്ടമ്മയാണ്. കടുങ്ങല്ലൂർ ഗ്രാമത്തിൽ നിന്നുള്ള ഇവർ തൃശ്ശൂർ ജില്ലയിൽ നിന്നുള്ളവളാണ്. സങ്കീർണ്ണമായ വിട്ടുമാറാത്ത പരിചരണ മാനേജ്‌മെന്റിനായുള്ള തുടർപരിശോധനയ്ക്കായി അവർ എത്തിയിരിക്കുന്നു. കഴിഞ്ഞ മൂന്ന് ആഴ്ചകളായി അവർക്ക് തുടർച്ചയായ ക്ഷീണവും ഇടവിട്ടുള്ള നേരിയ പനിയും അനുഭവപ്പെടുന്നു. പരിശോധനയിൽ, അവർക്ക് പനിയില്ല (afebrile), താപനില 98.4°F, രക്തസമ്മർദ്ദം 110/70 mmHg, ഹൃദയമിടിപ്പ് 88 bpm, ശ്വസന നിരക്ക് മിനിറ്റിൽ 18 തവണ. അവരുടെ ഭാരം 52 കിലോയും BMI 20.5 ഉം ആണ്. ഉദര പരിശോധനയിൽ വലത് മുകൾ ഭാഗത്ത് ഏകദേശം 5x4 സെന്റിമീറ്റർ വലിപ്പമുള്ളതും വേദനയില്ലാത്തതുമായ ഒരു മുഴ (mass) കണ്ടെത്തി. കാര്യമായ ലിംഫ് നോഡുകളുടെ വീക്കം (lymphadenopathy) ശ്രദ്ധയിൽപ്പെട്ടിട്ടില്ല. രോഗിക്ക് അടുത്തിടെ സങ്കീർണ്ണമായ ഉദര അർബുദം (abdominal neoplasm) സ്ഥിരീകരിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ട്. കൂടുതൽ ഓങ്കോളജിക്കൽ വിലയിരുത്തലിനും ചികിത്സയ്ക്കുമായി അവരെ ജില്ലാ ആശുപത്രിയിലേക്ക് റഫർ ചെയ്തിട്ടുണ്ട്. അവരുടെ ആശാ (ASHA) പ്രവർത്തക [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sujatha]] അവരുടെ ലക്ഷണങ്ങളും മരുന്നുകൾ കഴിക്കുന്നതിലെ കൃത്യതയും നിരീക്ഷിച്ചുകൊണ്ടിരിക്കുന്നു. രോഗിയുടെ പക്കൽ [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-KL-2023-001234]] ഉണ്ട്, അവരുടെ [[VOTER_ID|KL781234567]] ഫയലിലുണ്ട്. ഡിജിറ്റൽ ആരോഗ്യ റെക്കോർഡുകൾക്കായി അവരുടെ [[ABHA_ID|ABHA-KL-26-9876543210]] രജിസ്റ്റർ ചെയ്തിട്ടുണ്ട്. തുടർപരിശോധനയ്ക്കുള്ള കോൺടാക്റ്റ് നമ്പർ [[PHONE_NUMBER|9447012345]] ആണ്. അവരുടെ [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|House No. 12, St. Thomas Colony, Kadungallur]] സിസ്റ്റത്തിൽ രേഖപ്പെടുത്തിയിട്ടുണ്ട്. ജില്ലാ ആശുപത്രിയിലെ ചികിത്സാ ഡോക്ടർ Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Nair]] ആണ്. അവരുടെ [[MRN|MRN-KL-THIR-2024-0890]] ടെർഷ്യറി കെയർ ഫെസിലിറ്റിയുമായി ബന്ധിപ്പിച്ചിരിക്കുന്നു. സ്പെഷ്യലിസ്റ്റിന്റെ നിർദ്ദേശപ്രകാരം അൾട്രാസൗണ്ട്, സിടി സ്കാൻ എന്നിവയ്ക്ക് വിധേയമാകാൻ രോഗിക്ക് നിർദ്ദേശം നൽകിയിട്ടുണ്ട്. ശരീരത്തിൽ ജലാംശം നിലനിർത്തുന്നതിനെക്കുറിച്ചും എളുപ്പത്തിൽ ദഹിക്കുന്ന മൃദുവായ ഭക്ഷണക്രമത്തെക്കുറിച്ചും അവർക്ക് കൗൺസിലിംഗ് നൽകി. ഇന്ന് മുതൽ രണ്ടാഴ്ചയ്ക്ക് ശേഷം ഒരു തുടർപരിശോധന നിശ്ചയിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ട്.
generated_text_en:
PHC register — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Primary Health Centre, Chavakkad]] Village [[VILLAGE|Kadungallur]] District [[DISTRICT|Thrissur]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Annamma Thomas]] [[AGE|26]] [[GENDER|Female]] | Follow-up for complex chronic care

Patient is a 26-year-old female homemaker from Kadungallur village, Thrissur district, presenting for follow-up of her complex chronic care management. She reports persistent fatigue and intermittent low-grade fevers for the past three weeks. On examination, she is afebrile with a temperature of 98.4°F, blood pressure of 110/70 mmHg, heart rate of 88 bpm, and respiratory rate of 18 breaths per minute. Her weight is 52 kg with a BMI of 20.5. Abdominal examination reveals a palpable, non-tender mass in the right upper quadrant measuring approximately 5x4 cm. No significant lymphadenopathy is noted. The patient's medical history is significant for a recent diagnosis of a complex abdominal neoplasm. She has been referred to the District Hospital for further oncological evaluation and management. Her ASHA worker, [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sujatha]], has been monitoring her symptoms and medication adherence. The patient holds a [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-KL-2023-001234]] and her [[VOTER_ID|KL781234567]] is on file. For digital health records, her [[ABHA_ID|ABHA-KL-26-9876543210]] has been registered. Contact for follow-up is at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9447012345]]. Her [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|House No. 12, St. Thomas Colony, Kadungallur]] is documented in the system. The treating physician at the district hospital is Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Nair]]. Her [[MRN|MRN-KL-THIR-2024-0890]] is linked to the tertiary care facility. The patient is advised to undergo an ultrasound and CT scan as per the specialist's recommendation. She is counseled on maintaining hydration and a soft, easily digestible diet. A follow-up appointment is scheduled for two weeks from today.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Lab report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Regional Cancer Centre Imphal]] MRN [[MRN|RC-2024-0815-001]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]] [[AGE|33]] [[GENDER|Female]] Ordered by [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. L. Singh]]
[[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]] [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]] [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]] [[DISTRICT|Bishnupur]]
Patient from rural Bishnupur, Manipur, presents with persistent fatigue and intermittent low-grade fevers. Initial workup suggests a hematologic malignancy.

Test Name: Complete Blood Count with Differential
Reference Range: WBC: 4.0-11.0 x10^9/L, RBC: 4.2-5.9 x10^12/L, Hb: 12.0-15.5 g/dL, Platelets: 150-450 x10^9/L

Results:
WBC: 15.2 x10^9/L (High)
RBC: 3.8 x10^12/L (Low)
Hb: 9.1 g/dL (Low)
Platelets: 110 x10^9/L (Low)
Neutrophils: 75% (High)
Lymphocytes: 15% (Low)
Monocytes: 5% (Normal)
Eosinophils: 3% (Normal)
Basophils: 2% (Normal)

Test Name: Peripheral Blood Smear
Findings: Marked leukocytosis with a left shift. Presence of circulating blasts noted. Anemia and thrombocytopenia are confirmed. Morphology is suggestive of acute leukemia.

Test Name: Serum LDH
Result: 850 U/L (High)

Test Name: Serum Uric Acid
Result: 7.8 mg/dL (High)

Test Name: Coagulation Profile (PT/INR, aPTT)
PT: 14.5 seconds (Normal)
INR: 1.0 (Normal)
aPTT: 32.0 seconds (Normal)

Test Name: Comprehensive Metabolic Panel
Sodium: 138 mEq/L (Normal)
Potassium: 4.2 mEq/L (Normal)
Chloride: 101 mEq/L (Normal)
BUN: 18 mg/dL (Normal)
Creatinine: 0.9 mg/dL (Normal)
Glucose: 95 mg/dL (Normal)
Calcium: 8.5 mg/dL (Normal)
Total Protein: 6.8 g/dL (Low)
Albumin: 3.2 g/dL (Low)
Total Bilirubin: 1.1 mg/dL (Normal)
Direct Bilirubin: 0.3 mg/dL (Normal)
AST: 45 U/L (Normal)
ALT: 40 U/L (Normal)
Alkaline Phosphatase: 120 U/L (Normal)
LDH: 850 U/L (High)

Impression:
The laboratory findings are consistent with an acute leukemia, likely acute myeloid leukemia (AML) or acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL), given the high white blood cell count, anemia, thrombocytopenia, and presence of circulating blasts. Further immunophenotyping and cytogenetic analysis are required for definitive diagnosis and classification. The elevated LDH and uric acid suggest high cell turnover and risk of tumor lysis syndrome.

Plan:
1. Admit patient for further evaluation and management.
2. Initiate tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis with aggressive hydration and allopurinol.
3. Proceed with bone marrow aspiration and biopsy for definitive diagnosis.
4. Perform flow cytometry and cytogenetic studies on the bone marrow sample.
5. Consult with the hematology-oncology team for initiation of induction chemotherapy upon confirmation of diagnosis.
6. Counsel patient and family regarding the diagnosis and proposed treatment plan.

professional_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar is a vigilant rural security officer who excels at patrolling, crowd control and basic first aid, leveraging fluency in Marathi and Kannada to issue alerts, while they are curious about new safety tech and maintain a relaxed approach to paperwork that keeps them adaptable yet occasionally disorganized.
linguistic_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar speaks Marathi fluently and Kannada proficiently, can read simple notices in both languages, has a passive understanding of Hindi, and they are keen to improve literacy to handle more complex administrative tasks.
sports_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar avidly plays kabaddi with the village team, cheers for the Royal Challengers Bangalore in cricket, follows local football club FC Bengaluru United, and they organize spontaneous street cricket games during festivals, reflecting a competitive spirit and love for team camaraderie.
arts_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar enjoys listening to Marathi bhavgeet and Kannada folk songs, sketches bustling village scenes on old notebook paper, admires street murals in nearby towns, and they follow the music of Lata Mangeshkar and the films of Rajkumar, letting these artistic influences fuel their creative side.
travel_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar prefers short road trips to nearby hill stations like Mahabaleshwar and historic towns like Hampi, plans travel with friends on a modest budget, enjoys spontaneous train rides during festivals, and they dream of visiting Goa's beaches when finances allow.
culinary_persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar loves the tangy taste of Maharashtrian misal pav, the hearty texture of Karnataka's bisi bele bath, enjoys crunchy peanuts and spicy chivda at fairs, and they prefer homemade poha for breakfast, though they rarely cook themselves and often indulge in street food bazaars.
persona: Ayyamma Vaalikar blends vigilant rural security work with a love for folk music, spontaneous games, and modest savings, juggling curiosity and a laid‑back streak while dreaming of a supervisory role.
cultural_background: Ayyamma hails from the rural outskirts of Belgaum in Karnataka, a region where Marathi-speaking communities coexist with Kannada culture. Her family follows traditional agrarian customs, celebrates festivals like Ganesh Chaturthi and Ugadi, and maintains the strong communal bond typical of village life in the Deccan plateau.
linguistic_background: Her mother tongue is Marathi, which she speaks fluently and understands well in both spoken and basic written form. She is also proficient in Kannada, allowing her to communicate effectively with neighbours and local authorities. Due to limited formal schooling (below primary), her literacy in Marathi and Kannada is functional but not advanced; she can read simple notices and fill out basic security logs. She has a passive understanding of Hindi but does not read or write it.
skills_and_expertise: Ayyamma has practical experience as a security officer in a rural setting. She is skilled in vigilant observation, patrolling, crowd control, and basic first aid. She can handle simple security equipment, maintain incident logs, and coordinate with local police when needed. Her multilingual ability allows her to communicate warnings and instructions in Marathi and Kannada effectively. Additionally, she possesses basic knowledge of village safety protocols and emergency response.

hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys attending village fairs, listening to Marathi bhavgeet and Kannada folk songs, and dancing during festivals. Ayyamma is curious about street art and loves sketching simple drawings of everyday scenes. She also participates in outdoor games like kabaddi and enjoys spontaneous group outings with friends. Occasionally, she watches Bollywood movies and reads comic strips for light entertainment.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Attending village fairs', 'Listening to Marathi bhavgeet and Kannada folk songs', 'Dancing during festivals', 'Sketching everyday scenes', 'Playing kabaddi', 'Spontaneous group outings with friends', 'Watching Bollywood movies', 'Reading comic strips']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Ayyamma aspires to become a senior security supervisor, seeking formal training and certification in safety management. She aims to improve her literacy in Marathi and Kannada to handle administrative duties more confidently. In the long term, she hopes to secure a stable position within a government security department, ensuring a steady income for her family while contributing to community safety.
persona_clinical_summary: A 35-year-old female security officer from rural Karnataka, currently married, with below-primary education. She speaks Marathi and Kannada, works in a rural security role, and resides in Belgaum district. Clinically, she has modest savings and a laid-back lifestyle, with no reported substance use or chronic conditions. system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 35
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Security Officer
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Belgaum
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 35-year-old female security officer from rural Karnataka, currently married, with below-primary education. She speaks Marathi and Kannada, works in a rural security role, and resides in Belgaum district. Clinically, she has modest savings and a laid-back lifestyle, with no reported substance use or chronic conditions. Her health behaviors include occasional physical activity through kabaddi and a diet rich in local staples like misal pav and bisi bele bath.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
शस्त्रक्रिया नोंद — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Belgaum]] प्रवेश [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1025]] विभाग [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Sunita Patil]] [[AGE|35]] [[GENDER|Female]] शल्यचिकित्सक [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Kulkarni]]
प्रक्रिया / निरीक्षणे: रुग्णाला तीन दिवसांपासून उजव्या बाजूच्या पोटाच्या खालच्या भागात वेदना, ताप आणि उलट्यांचा इतिहास होता. तपासणीत, उजव्या इलियाक फोसा भागात वेदना जाणवत होत्या आणि स्नायू ताणलेले होते. अल्ट्रासाऊंडमध्ये 6 सेमीचा अपेंडिसीयल मास दिसून आला. इंटरव्हल अपेंडिसेक्टोमी करण्याचा निर्णय घेण्यात आला. रुग्णाला ऑपरेशन थिएटरमध्ये नेण्यात आले आणि जनरल ॲनेस्थेशिया दिल्यानंतर, मानक मॅकबर्नी इन्सिजन (चीरा) देण्यात आला. अपेंडिक्सला सूज आली होती आणि ते मोबिलाइझ करण्यात आले. मेसोअपेंडिक्सला 2-0 ॲबसॉर्बेबल स्युचर्सने बांधले गेले. त्यानंतर अपेंडिक्स त्याच्या तळापासून काढले गेले. स्टंपला पर्स-स्ट्रिंग स्युचरने आत वळवले गेले आणि ओमेंटमने बळकट केले गेले. जखम नॉर्मल सलाईनने स्वच्छ केली गेली आणि थरांमध्ये बंद केली गेली. रुग्णाने ही प्रक्रिया चांगल्या प्रकारे सहन केली आणि स्थिर स्थितीत रिकव्हरी रूममध्ये हलवण्यात आले.
शस्त्रक्रियेनंतरची योजना: रुग्णाचे 24 तास निरीक्षण केले जाईल. आवश्यकतेनुसार वेदनाशामक औषधे दिली जातील. आहार सहन होईल तसा सुरू केला जाईल. जर रुग्णाला ताप नसेल आणि वेदना नियंत्रित असतील, तर तिसऱ्या शस्त्रक्रियेनंतरच्या दिवशी डिस्चार्ज दिला जाईल.
[[BED_NUMBER|15]]
[[MRN|MRN-2024-1025-001]]
[[PATIENT_ID|PID-78901]]
[[RELATIVE_NAME|Vikram Patil]]
generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Belgaum]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1025]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Sunita Patil]] [[AGE|35]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Kulkarni]]
Procedure / findings: The patient presented with a history of right lower quadrant abdominal pain for three days, associated with fever and vomiting. On examination, she was tender in the right iliac fossa with guarding. An ultrasound revealed a 6 cm appendiceal mass. The decision was made for an interval appendectomy. The patient was taken to the operating theatre, and after induction of general anesthesia, a standard McBurney incision was made. The appendix was found to be inflamed and was mobilized. The mesoappendix was ligated with 2-0 absorbable sutures. The appendix was then resected at the base. The stump was inverted with a purse-string suture and reinforced with omentum. The wound was irrigated with normal saline, and the layers were closed. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was shifted to the recovery room in stable condition.
Post-operative plan: The patient will be observed for 24 hours. Analgesics will be provided as needed. Diet will be started as tolerated. She will be discharged on the third post-operative day if she remains afebrile and her pain is controlled.
[[BED_NUMBER|15]]
[[MRN|MRN-2024-1025-001]]
[[PATIENT_ID|PID-78901]]
[[RELATIVE_NAME|Vikram Patil]]
judge_reasoning: Marathi Devanagari prose with valid surgical content; all entity TYPEs allowed; persona/clinical fit and surrogate plausibility intact; no script or length issues.

professional_persona: Aruna Thakubiya, a 21‑year‑old home‑based catering enthusiast, combines meticulous budgeting, multilingual communication, and skilled preparation of traditional Nepal‑Assam dishes to deliver authentic meals while methodically planning to grow a small catering service.
linguistic_persona: Aruna Thakubiya speaks Nepali fluently at home, converses comfortably in Hindi and Assamese for daily errands, and navigates information through radio and mobile audio, despite being illiterate.
sports_persona: Aruna Thakubiya follows Indian Premier League cricket, cheering for the Kolkata Knight Riders, enjoys watching Assam's Ranji cricket matches, and maintains light fitness by taking early morning walks in her garden and occasional yoga sessions.
arts_persona: Aruna Thakubiya relishes folk melodies on bansuri and dhol, frequently tunes into performances by Zubeen Garg and Nepali folk singer Narayan Gopal, and enjoys watching devotional dramas on regional TV.
travel_persona: Aruna Thakubiya has toured Guwahati and Sikkim, briefly visited Delhi for a cultural fest, and aspires to explore Kathmandu and Manali, preferring budget‑friendly travel arranged via radio tips and community word‑of‑mouth.
culinary_persona: Aruna Thakubiya delights in cooking momos, sel roti, masor tenga, and pitha, expertly blending mustard seeds, ginger, and bamboo shoots into her meals, and enjoys savoring the vibrant flavors of these traditional dishes with her family.
persona: Aruna Thakubiya, a 21‑year‑old organized dream‑chaser, blends a love for cooking, quiet gardening, and community rituals while juggling modest finances and a stubborn habit of checking radio news every hour.
cultural_background: Aruna hails from a Nepali-speaking Gorkha community in Dibrugarh, Assam. Her family maintains Nepali customs such as celebrating Dashain and Tihar, while also participating in Assamese cultural practices like Bihu. Being Hindu, she follows rituals and worships deities common to both Nepali and Assamese Hindu traditions. Growing up in an urban setting, she navigates a blend of Nepali, Assamese, and broader Indian urban culture.
linguistic_background: Linguistic background: Aruna’s first language is Nepali, which she speaks fluently at home. She also communicates in Hindi and Assamese for daily interactions in the community and marketplace. Being illiterate, she cannot read or write in any of these languages, but she can recognize basic symbols and uses audio-visual media, such as radio and TV, to stay informed.
skills_and_expertise: Aruna is skilled in preparing Nepali and Assamese home-cooked meals, managing household finances through simple budgeting, caring for children, and maintaining a tidy home. She is adept at basic sewing and weaving, using a handloom for traditional garments. Her conversational abilities in Nepali, Hindi, and Assamese allow her to interact smoothly with neighbors and shopkeepers. She also knows how to operate a basic mobile phone for communication.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Cooking Nepali dishes', 'Cooking Assamese dishes', 'Household budgeting', 'Child care', 'Basic sewing', 'Handloom weaving', 'Conversational Nepali', 'Conversational Hindi', 'Conversational Assamese', 'Mobile phone operation']
hobbies_and_interests: Aruna enjoys cooking traditional Nepali and Assamese dishes for family gatherings, listening to folk music, especially the melodies of the bansuri and dhol, and watching devotional TV programs. She likes to spend quiet time in her garden, tending to potted plants, and occasionally practices simple knitting. On weekends, she participates in community Bihu dances and helps organize local festivals.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Cooking traditional dishes', 'Listening to folk music', 'Watching devotional TV shows', 'Gardening', 'Simple knitting', 'Participating in Bihu dance', 'Helping organize local festivals']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Aruna aspires to enhance her household management skills and hopes to learn basic reading and writing in Nepali and Hindi through community adult education classes. She dreams of starting a small home-based catering service offering Nepali and Assamese delicacies, which would provide additional income for her family. Over the next few years, she wishes to become a respected community volunteer, helping organize festivals and supporting other women in similar circumstances.
persona_clinical_summary: A 21-year-old female from Dibrugarh, Assam, who is currently married and has no formal occupation, reports limited education and is illiterate. She speaks Nepali as her first language, with functional Hindi and Assamese, and resides in an urban zone. Her lifestyle includes modest financial management, traditional cooking of Nepali-Assamese dishes, and regular consumption of local news via radio.

[[PATIENT_NAME|Sunita Sharma]] [[AGE|21]] [[GENDER|Female]] शल्यचिकित्सक [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anup Das]]
प्रक्रिया / निष्कर्ष: बिरामी 24 घण्टादेखि पेटको तल्लो दाहिने भागमा तीव्र दुखाइका साथ आएका थिए। परीक्षण गर्दा, म्याक बर्निस बिन्दुमा tenderness र guarding देखिएको थियो। अल्ट्रासाउन्डले 5.2 cm को appendiceal mass देखाएको थियो। Laparoscopic appendectomy गर्ने निर्णय गरियो। सामान्य एनेस्थेसिया अन्तर्गत प्रक्रिया सम्पन्न गरियो। Appendix सुन्निएको र tip मा perforated भएको पाइयो। यसलाई dissect गरियो, endoloops ले ligated गरियो, र endobag मा निकालियो। Stump लाई normal saline ले irrigate गरियो। पेट भित्र कुनै abscess देखिएन। Port sites हरू बन्द गरियो। अनुमानित रक्तस्राव न्यूनतम थियो। बिरामीले प्रक्रिया राम्रोसँग सहन गरे। शल्यक्रिया पछिको योजनामा 24 घण्टासम्म observation, IV fluids, र analgesics समावेश छन्। [[BED_NUMBER|12]] [[MRN|MRN-2024-1105-001]] [[PATIENT_ID|PID-2105]] [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Sharma]]

Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Dibrugarh Civil Hospital]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1105]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Sunita Sharma]] [[AGE|21]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anup Das]]
Procedure / findings: Patient presented with acute right lower quadrant abdominal pain for 24 hours. On examination, tenderness was noted at McBurney's point with guarding. Ultrasound revealed a 5.2 cm appendiceal mass. Decision was made for laparoscopic appendectomy. Procedure was performed under general anesthesia. The appendix was found to be inflamed and perforated at the tip. It was dissected, ligated with endoloops, and removed in an endobag. The stump was irrigated with normal saline. No intra-abdominal abscess was noted. The port sites were closed. Estimated blood loss was minimal. The patient tolerated the procedure well. Post-operative plan includes observation for 24 hours, IV fluids, and analgesics. [[BED_NUMBER|12]] [[MRN|MRN-2024-1105-001]] [[PATIENT_ID|PID-2105]] [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Sharma]]

persona: Sarsati Patra is a lively 20‑year‑old artisan who juggles temple sketches, kitchen experiments, and spontaneous beach walks, often losing track of time but always greeting friends with a bright smile.
cultural_background: Sarsati hails from the coastal rural belt of Puri in Odisha, a region steeped in Jagannath temple traditions, vibrant folk arts such as Pattachitra and Odissi dance, and a close‑knit village life where community gatherings revolve around festivals and agricultural cycles.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):

generated_text:
ପ୍ରେସକ୍ରିପସନ୍ — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|SCB Medical College Hospital]]
ରୋଗୀ [[PATIENT_NAME|Rashmi Pradhan]], [[AGE|20]] / [[GENDER|Female]], MRN [[MRN|SCB-2024-0815-001]]
ଡାକ୍ତର [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. K. Mishra]]
ଫୋନ୍: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9437981234]]
ଠିକଣା: [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|Village: Gobindpur, Post: Puri, District: Puri, Odisha]]
ABHA ID: [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
ରୋଗୀ ID: [[PATIENT_ID|PID-SCB-2024-0815-001]]
ଜିଲ୍ଲା: [[DISTRICT|Puri]]

ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଅଭିଯୋଗ:
ରୋଗୀ ଗତ ତିନି ମାସ ଧରି କ୍ରମାଗତ କ୍ଲାନ୍ତି, ମଝିରେ ମଝିରେ ସ୍ୱଳ୍ପ ଜ୍ୱର ଏବଂ ଓଜନରେ ଯଥେଷ୍ଟ ହ୍ରାସ ସହିତ ଆସିଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ଶୁଖିଲା କାଶ ଏବଂ ବେଳେବେଳେ ରାତିରେ ଝାଳ ବୋହିବା ବିଷୟରେ ଜଣାଇଛନ୍ତି। କୌଣସି ଜଣାଶୁଣା ସଂକ୍ରମିତ ସମ୍ପର୍କ ନାହିଁ।

କ୍ଲିନିକାଲ୍ ଅନୁସନ୍ଧାନ:
ପରୀକ୍ଷା ସମୟରେ, ରୋଗୀ ଫିକା ଦେଖାଯାଉଛନ୍ତି, ନାଡ଼ିର ଗତି ମିନିଟ୍ ପ୍ରତି 102, ରକ୍ତଚାପ 110/70 mmHg ଏବଂ ତାପମାତ୍ରା 99.4°F ଅଛି। ଶ୍ୱାସକ୍ରିୟା ପ୍ରଣାଳୀରେ ଉଭୟ ପାର୍ଶ୍ୱରେ କ୍ରେପିଟେସନ୍ ଦେଖାଯାଉଛି। ପେଟ ନରମ ଅଛି ଏବଂ କୌଣସି ଅଙ୍ଗ ବୃଦ୍ଧି ହୋଇନାହିଁ। ପ୍ରାରମ୍ଭିକ ରକ୍ତ ପରୀକ୍ଷା ସାମାନ୍ୟ ନର୍ମୋସାଇଟିକ୍ ଆନିମିଆ (Hb 9.8 g/dL) ଏବଂ ବର୍ଦ୍ଧିତ ESR ଦର୍ଶାଉଛି। ଛାତିର ଏକ୍ସ-ରେ ଏକ ଦକ୍ଷିଣ ହିଲାର ମାସ୍ (mass) ଦର୍ଶାଉଛି।

ଅନୁସନ୍ଧାନ:
କଣ୍ଟ୍ରାଷ୍ଟ ସହିତ ଛାତିର CT ସ୍କାନ୍ ଦକ୍ଷିଣ ଉପର ଲୋବ୍‌ରେ 4.5 cm ମାସ୍ ଏବଂ ମିଡିଆଷ୍ଟିନାଲ୍ ଲିମ୍ଫାଡେନୋପାଥି ଦର୍ଶାଉଛି। AFB ପାଇଁ କଫ ପରୀକ୍ଷା ନକାରାତ୍ମକ ଅଟେ। ସେରମ୍ LDH ବୃଦ୍ଧି ପାଇଛି।

ନିଦାନ:
ଷ୍ଟେଜ୍ IIIA ନନ୍-ସ୍ମଲ୍ ସେଲ୍ ଲଙ୍ଗ୍ କ୍ୟାନ୍ସର (NSCLC), ଆଡେନୋକାର୍ସିନୋମା।

ଯୋଜନା:
1. ଅଧିକ ମୂଲ୍ୟାଙ୍କନ ଏବଂ ଷ୍ଟେଜିଂ ୱାର୍କଅପ୍ ପାଇଁ ଅଙ୍କୋଲୋଜି ବିଭାଗକୁ ପଠାଇବା।
2. ମେଟାବୋଲିକ୍ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକଳାପ ଏବଂ ଦୂରବର୍ତ୍ତୀ ମେଟାଷ୍ଟାସିସ୍ ଆକଳନ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ବେସଲାଇନ୍ PET-CT ସ୍କାନ୍।
3. EGFR, ALK ଏବଂ ROS1 ମ୍ୟୁଟେସନ୍ ପାଇଁ ଜେନେଟିକ୍ ପରୀକ୍ଷା।
4. ପୁଷ୍ଟିକର ସହାୟତା ଏବଂ ପରାମର୍ଶ।
5. ଏକ ସପ୍ତାହ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ଫଲୋ-ଅପ୍ ଆପଏଣ୍ଟମେଣ୍ଟ ନିର୍ଦ୍ଧାରିତ ହୋଇଛି।

ପ୍ରେସକ୍ରିପସନ୍:
- ପାରାସিটାମୋଲ୍ 500 mg, ଜ୍ୱର ପାଇଁ ଆବଶ୍ୟକ ଅନୁଯାୟୀ ଦିନକୁ ତିନିଥର ଗୋଟିଏ ଟାବଲେଟ୍ ପାଟିରେ।
- ଓଣ୍ଡାନସେଟ୍ରନ୍ 4 mg, ବାନ୍ତି ପାଇଁ ଆବଶ୍ୟକ ଅନୁଯାୟୀ ଦିନକୁ ଦୁଇଥର ଗୋଟିଏ ଟାବଲେଟ୍ ପାଟିରେ।
- ଫୋଲିକ୍ ଏସିଡ୍ 5 mg, ଦିନକୁ ଥରେ ଗୋଟିଏ ଟାବଲେଟ୍ ପାଟିରେ।
- ଭିଟାମିନ୍ B12 500 mcg, ଦିନକୁ ଥରେ ଗୋଟିଏ ଟାବଲେଟ୍ ପାଟିରେ।
- ଆଇରନ୍ ଏବଂ ଫୋଲିକ୍ ଏସିଡ୍ ଟାବଲେଟ୍, ଦିନକୁ ଥରେ ଗୋଟିଏ ଟାବଲେଟ୍ ପାଟିରେ।
- ପୁଷ୍ଟିକର ପରିପୂରକ (ଯଥା: Ensure) ଦିନକୁ ଦୁଇଥର।

ନିର୍ଦ୍ଦେଶାବଳୀ:
ଦୟାକରି ଅଙ୍କୋଲୋଜି ବିଭାଗ ସହିତ ଫଲୋ-ଅପ୍ କରନ୍ତୁ। ପ୍ରୋଟିନ୍ ଏବଂ କ୍ୟାଲୋରୀରେ ଭରପୂର ଏକ ସନ୍ତୁଳିତ ଆହାର ଗ୍ରହଣ କରନ୍ତୁ। ଧୂମପାନ ଏବଂ ପରୋକ୍ଷ ଧୂମପାନରୁ ଦୂରେଇ ରୁହନ୍ତୁ। କୌଣସି ନୂତନ ଲକ୍ଷଣ ଦେଖାଦେଲେ ତୁରନ୍ତ ଜଣାନ୍ତୁ।
generated_text_en:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|SCB Medical College Hospital]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Rashmi Pradhan]], [[AGE|20]] / [[GENDER|Female]], MRN [[MRN|SCB-2024-0815-001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. K. Mishra]]
Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9437981234]]
Address: [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|Village: Gobindpur, Post: Puri, District: Puri, Odisha]]
ABHA ID: [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
Patient ID: [[PATIENT_ID|PID-SCB-2024-0815-001]]
District: [[DISTRICT|Puri]]

Chief Complaint:
Patient presents with persistent fatigue, intermittent low-grade fever, and significant weight loss over the past three months. She reports a non-productive cough and occasional night sweats. No known sick contacts.

Clinical Findings:
On examination, the patient is pale, with a pulse of 102/min, blood pressure of 110/70 mmHg, and temperature of 99.4°F. Respiratory system shows bilateral crepitations. Abdomen is soft with no organomegaly. Initial blood work reveals mild normocytic anemia (Hb 9.8 g/dL) and elevated ESR. Chest X-ray shows a right hilar mass.

Investigations:
CT scan of chest with contrast reveals a 4.5 cm mass in the right upper lobe with mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Sputum for AFB is negative. Serum LDH is elevated.

Diagnosis:
Stage IIIA Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC), Adenocarcinoma.

Plan:
1. Referral to Oncology Department for further evaluation and staging workup.
2. Baseline PET-CT scan to assess metabolic activity and distant metastases.
3. Genetic testing for EGFR, ALK, and ROS1 mutations.
4. Nutritional support and counseling.
5. Follow-up appointment scheduled in one week.

Prescription:
- Paracetamol 500 mg, one tablet orally three times a day as needed for fever.
- Ondansetron 4 mg, one tablet orally twice a day as needed for nausea.
- Folic acid 5 mg, one tablet orally once daily.
- Vitamin B12 500 mcg, one tablet orally once daily.
- Iron and Folic Acid tablet, one tablet orally once daily.
- Nutritional supplement (e.g., Ensure) twice daily.

Instructions:
Please follow up with the Oncology department. Maintain a balanced diet rich in protein and calories. Avoid smoking and exposure to secondhand smoke. Report any new symptoms immediately.

professional_persona: Swarna Kanr, a retired village elder and cultural custodian, channels her decades of expertise in traditional household management, Phulkari embroidery, and folk storytelling into mentoring grandchildren, balancing meticulous organization with a vivid imagination and a gentle yet assertive demeanor, and they bring the same disciplined creativity to every community activity they join.
linguistic_persona: Swarna Kanr, a lifelong oral storyteller, speaks fluent Punjabi, understands spoken Hindi in daily conversations, yet cannot read Gurmukhi, Hindi or English scripts, relying on memory and auditory learning for all communication, and they cherish the power of spoken word above written text.
sports_persona: Swarna Kanr enjoys watching Kabaddi matches on TV, follows the Indian Premier League and roots for the Delhi Capitals, takes a leisurely morning walk around the village field, and occasionally joins community Giddha sessions as a lively form of exercise.
arts_persona: Swarna Kanr passionately weaves intricate Phulkari embroidery on cloth, leads spirited Giddha dances during village celebrations, sings devotional bhajans each sunrise, and cherishes listening to legendary folk singers like Surinder Kaur and Jagjit Singh.
travel_persona: Swarna Kanr prefers short pilgrimages to nearby holy sites like Amritsar’s Golden Temple and seasonal trips to Shimla for fresh air, plans trips well in advance with family, travels by train or shared auto, and avoids long‑haul flights due to health concerns.
culinary_persona: Swarna Kanr expertly prepares makki di roti, sarson da saag, and buttery lassi, enjoys the aromatic blend of mustard greens and jaggery, prefers home‑grown vegetables from her kitchen garden, and while she no longer cooks daily, she delights in sharing her recipes during family gatherings.
persona: Swarna Kanr, a vivacious 73‑year‑old keeper of oral tales and Phulkari art, balances frugal living with generous festival splurges, manages health aches with herbal remedies, and radiates curiosity through endless storytelling.
cultural_background: Swarna hails from a small village near Rupnagar in the rural heartland of Punjab. She belongs to a Punjabi Hindu family that has lived in the region for generations, steeped in Punjabi folk traditions, agricultural life, and community festivals such as Baisakhi, Lohri and Teeyan. Her household follows joint‑family customs, with strong emphasis on hospitality, respect for elders and the preservation of oral history.
linguistic_background: Swarna's mother tongue is Punjabi; she speaks Punjabi fluently and understands its nuances, idioms and proverbs. Because she never attended school, she cannot read or write Gurmukhi, Hindi or English. She can follow simple spoken Hindi in daily conversations and understands basic instructions from television or neighbours, but she does not read Hindi script. Her linguistic skills are rooted in oral tradition, storytelling and memorised devotional chants.
skills_and_expertise: Swarna excels in managing a Punjabi household, preparing traditional dishes such as makki di roti, sarson da saag, and lassi. She is proficient in Phulkari embroidery, creating intricate needlework for family shawls. She leads Giddha dance performances during festivals, sings devotional bhajans, and narrates folk stories (kisse) to younger relatives. She knows home remedies using local herbs, cultivates a kitchen garden with seasonal vegetables, and mediates family disputes with calm authority.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Punjabi cooking", "Phulkari embroidery", "Giddha dance", "Bhajan singing", "Oral storytelling (kisse)", "Herbal home remedies", "Kitchen gardening", "Family budgeting", "Community dispute mediation"]
hobbies_and_interests: Swarna enjoys listening to Punjabi folk music on the radio, singing bhajans during early mornings, and dancing Giddha at community gatherings. She spends afternoons tending to her kitchen garden, weaving phulkari on small cloth pieces, and recounting folk tales to grandchildren. She also likes watching devotional TV shows and occasionally knitting simple shawls for charity.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Punjabi folk music", "Singing bhajans", "Giddha dancing", "Kitchen gardening", "Phulkari stitching", "Storytelling to grandchildren", "Watching devotional TV shows", "Knitting simple shawls"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Although retired, Swarna aims to preserve her family’s cultural heritage by teaching her grandchildren Punjabi cooking, Phulkari stitching and folk songs. She wishes to document several family kisse on audio recordings for future generations. She also hopes to stay socially active by volunteering at the village temple’s senior volunteers’ group and learning basic smartphone use to keep in touch with distant relatives.
persona_clinical_summary: A 73-year-old female from rural Punjab, currently married, with no formal occupation and illiterate education. She speaks Punjabi fluently and follows simple spoken Hindi. Her lifestyle includes frugal living, managing health with herbal remedies, and participating in community festivals.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

user_prompt:
Write one complete Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.

PLACEMENT (device/network): Put IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, and MAC_ADDRESS exactly once each in a short session/metadata header. Do NOT repeat them in chat turns. Chat turns use patient/doctor/phone/email/appointment only.
Also put AGE and GENDER exactly once in the session header (e.g. Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]). Do NOT re-tag AGE/GENDER/PHONE/EMAIL/HOSPITAL_NAME in every turn.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 73
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Punjab
- district: Rupnagar
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 73-year-old female from rural Punjab, currently married, with no formal occupation and illiterate education. She speaks Punjabi fluently and follows simple spoken Hindi. Her lifestyle includes frugal living, managing health with herbal remedies, and participating in community festivals.

Document format guide for Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton — put demographics + device/network IDs ONLY in a short
session header (once each), then keep chat turns free of ID/metadata spam:
--- session ---
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|34]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|...]] Portal [[URL|https://tele.example.in/visit]]
Client IP [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] Device IMEI [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
MAC [[MAC_ADDRESS|A1:B2:C3:D4:E5:F6]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|...]] Hospital [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
--- chat ---
Patient: <symptom turn — no AGE/GENDER/PHONE re-tags>
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]: <advice turn>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- IP_ADDRESS — IP Address e.g. [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
- URL — URL (Personal Website/Profile) e.g. [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
- IMEI_NUMBER — IMEI Number e.g. [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
- MAC_ADDRESS — MAC Address e.g. [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: Hello Jaswinder ji. Thank you for sharing this. A cough with tiredness and fever for two months needs to be checked properly, especially with your history of diabetes. I am sending you a prescription for a chest X-ray and some blood tests. Please go to the Civil Hospital in Ropar and get these done.
Patient: Okay doctor. I will go. What about my diabetes medicine? Should I change it?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: Do not change your diabetes medicine on your own. Continue taking your current dose. We will review all your reports, including the X-ray and blood tests, after you get them done. Based on those, we can decide the next steps for both your cough and your diabetes. Please try to get the tests done within the next week.
Patient: I will try. Is there anything else I should do right now?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: For now, please get adequate rest and drink plenty of water. Avoid any heavy work. If your fever increases or you feel very unwell, you should go to the Emergency department at the Civil Hospital immediately. We will schedule a follow-up call after you have your reports.
Patient: Thank you doctor. I will do that.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]: You are welcome, Jaswinder ji. Take care.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

user_prompt:
Write one complete Automated SMS Notifications for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Sanskrit (code=sa, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.

LENGTH HARD LIMIT: entire SMS ≤ ~500 characters, but you MUST still include every mandatory entity tag below at least once (compact tags OK).

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 19
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Uttar Pradesh
- district: Kanpur Nagar
- first_language: Sanskrit
- clinical_persona_summary: A 19‑year‑old male from Kanpur Nagar, Uttar Pradesh, with a matriculation education, currently not employed. He resides in an urban setting and speaks Sanskrit as his first language, with functional English proficiency. His lifestyle includes occasional anxiety, yoga practice, and a preference for street food, while he is unmarried.

Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]. MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-001]].

MANDATORY inline entities (6 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

travel_persona: Narendra Rathinam prefers short weekend getaways to nearby hill stations like Ooty for cool breezes, enjoys occasional trips to the coastal town of Kanyakumari to watch sunrise over the seas, and dreams of attending a construction workshop in Bangalore to broaden his technical skills, balancing travel with work commitments and family responsibilities; they also plan to visit historic temples to study architectural details.
culinary_persona: Narendra Rathinam delights in traditional South Indian fare, favoring idli‑dosa paired with coconut chutney and sambar, relishes spicy Chettinad chicken curry with tamarind, enjoys a cup of filter coffee with a hint of cardamom, and appreciates the aroma of fresh mangoes and jackfruit during seasonal harvests; they often savor these dishes at neighborhood stalls rather than cooking at home.
persona: Narendra Rathinam blends a mason’s grit with an artist’s eye, balancing hard‑working ambition, rhythmic devotion, and a love for community traditions; they navigate life with curiosity and a touch of practical ‘jugaad’.
cultural_background: Narendra hails from a rural community near Madurai in Tamil Nadu. He grew up in a traditional Tamil household where family ties, temple festivals, and agrarian rhythms shape daily life. The local culture blends Tamil folk arts, such as karagattam and bharatanatyam, with the practical ethos of construction work that many villagers take up. While primarily Tamil, he is also exposed to Telugu customs through neighboring communities, giving him a modest cross‑cultural familiarity.
linguistic_background: Narendra is a native Tamil speaker with full literacy in Tamil. He acquired functional proficiency in Telugu through daily interaction with migrant workers, allowing him to converse, understand basic Telugu instructions, though reading and writing skills are modest. His formal schooling only up to matric provides him basic literacy in Hindi and English, sufficient for reading work orders, safety signs, and simple communication but not advanced fluency.
skills_and_expertise: Narendra has hands‑on experience in masonry, bricklaying, concrete mixing, and basic carpentry, honed from years of working on residential and small commercial projects. He is adept at reading construction drawings at a practical level, measuring dimensions accurately, and operating hand tools such as trowels, hammers, and power drills. His work also includes site safety awareness, teamwork, and problem‑solving using low‑cost ‘jugaad’ methods. Physical stamina and reliability in demanding schedules are notable strengths.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Masonry", "Bricklaying", "Concrete Mixing", "Basic Carpentry", "Construction Drawing Interpretation", "Dimensional Measuring", "Hand Tool Operation", "Site Safety Awareness", "Team Coordination", "Jugaad Problem Solving", "Physical Stamina"]
hobbies_and_interests: Narendra enjoys listening to Carnatic music and choral renditions of Tamil devotional songs, especially during temple festivals. He often sketches traditional temple architecture in a small notebook, an outlet for his artistic curiosity. In his spare time, he plays the thavil (a traditional drum) with local youth groups, watches Tamil movies, and reads mythological epics like the Ramayana. He also likes to experiment with simple wood‑carving and participates in community cricket matches, balancing social interaction with moments of solitary reflection.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Carnatic music", "Sketching temple architecture", "Playing the thavil", "Watching Tamil movies", "Reading mythological epics", "Wood carving", "Participating in cricket matches", "Attending temple festivals", "Exploring new ideas through community workshops"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Narendra aspires to transition from day‑labour to a supervisory role within the next few years. He aims to acquire a certified diploma in civil construction through a local ITI program, which would enable him to become a foreman or site supervisor. Ultimately, he dreams of establishing his own small construction firm, employing fellow village youth, and delivering quality, affordable housing that respects traditional Tamil aesthetics. Financial stability would also allow him to support his parents and invest in education for future generations.
persona_clinical_summary: A 29-year-old male from rural Tamil Nadu, Madurai district, works as a building construction labourer with a matriculation education. He is never married, speaks Tamil as his first language, and has functional Telugu proficiency. His lifestyle includes physical labor, occasional cricket, and a diet rich in traditional South Indian foods.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER
