Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 29
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: Building Construction Labourers, Other
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Rural
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Madurai
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: A 29-year-old male from rural Tamil Nadu, Madurai district, works as a building construction labourer with a matriculation education. He is never married, speaks Tamil as his first language, and has functional Telugu proficiency. His lifestyle includes physical labor, occasional cricket, and a diet rich in traditional South Indian foods.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Rajaji Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. K. Vijay]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|R. Murugan]], [[AGE|29]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Madurai]]
Reason: Persistent fever and joint pain for evaluation and management.

அன்புள்ள ஐயா/அம்மா,

நாங்கள் மேலே குறிப்பிட்ட நோயாளி, திரு. ஆர். முருகுன், 29 வயது ஆண் கட்டுமானத் தொழிலாளி, உங்கள் நிபுணர் கருத்திற்காக பரிந்துரைக்கிறோம். நோயாளி கடந்த மூன்று வாரங்களாக விட்டு விட்டு வரும் அதிக காய்ச்சல், முழங்கால்கள் மற்றும் மணிக்கட்டுகள் உள்ளிட்ட பல மூட்டுகளில் கடுமையான வலி மற்றும் வீக்கம், உடல் வலி மற்றும் சோர்வு ஆகியவற்றைக் கொண்டுள்ளார். பரிசோதனையில், அவருக்கு 102.4°F காய்ச்சல் உள்ளது. பல மூட்டுகளில் வீக்கத்திற்கான அறிகுறிகள் மற்றும் இயக்கக் கட்டுப்பாடு காணப்படுகின்றன. ஆரம்பகட்ட இரத்தப் பரிசோதனையில் அதிகப்படியான அழற்சி குறிகாட்டிகள் (ESR 85 mm/hr, CRP 12 mg/dL) மற்றும் லேசான வெள்ளை இரத்த அணுக்களின் எண்ணிக்கை அதிகரிப்பு ஆகியவை தெரியவந்துள்ளது. மருத்துவ நிலை ஒரு முறையான அழற்சி நிலையைச் சுட்டிக்காட்டுகிறது. நாங்கள் ஒரு உறுதியான நோயறிதல் மற்றும் பொருத்தமான சிகிச்சையைத் தொடங்க உங்கள் மதிப்பீட்டைப் பாராட்டுகிறோம்.

கூடுதல் தகவல்கள் அல்லது தெளிவுபடுத்தலுக்கு, தயவுசெய்து [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] இல் எங்களை தொடர்பு கொள்ளவும். நோயாளியின் தந்தை திரு. எஸ். ரமேஷ், அதே எண்ணைத் தொடர்பு கொள்ளலாம்.

நன்றி.

உண்மையுடன்,
டாக்டர் கே. விஜய
பொது மருத்துவம்
அரசு ராஜாஜி மருத்துவமனை, மதுரை
[[MRN|MRN-2024-0720-045]]
[[RELATIVE_NAME|S. Ramesh]]
[[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P1234567]]
[[DRIVING_LICENCE|TN45 20200012345]]
generated_text_en:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Rajaji Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. K. Vijay]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|R. Murugan]], [[AGE|29]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Madurai]]
Reason: Persistent fever and joint pain for evaluation and management.

Dear Sir/Madam,

We are referring the above-mentioned patient, Mr. R. Murugan, a 29-year-old male construction labourer, for your expert opinion. The patient presents with a history of intermittent high-grade fever, associated with severe pain and swelling in multiple joints, including the knees and wrists, for the past three weeks. He also reports generalized body aches and fatigue. On examination, he is febrile with a temperature of 102.4°F. Multiple joints show signs of inflammation with restricted range of motion. Initial blood investigations from our end reveal elevated inflammatory markers (ESR 85 mm/hr, CRP 12 mg/dL) and a mildly elevated white blood cell count. The clinical picture is suggestive of a systemic inflammatory condition. We would appreciate your assessment for a definitive diagnosis and initiation of appropriate treatment.

For any further information or clarification, please feel free to contact us at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. The patient's father, Mr. S. Ramesh, can be reached at the same number.

Thank you.

Sincerely,
Dr. K. Vijay
General Medicine
Government Rajaji Hospital, Madurai
[[MRN|MRN-2024-0720-045]]
[[RELATIVE_NAME|S. Ramesh]]
[[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P1234567]]
[[DRIVING_LICENCE|TN45 20200012345]]
judge_reasoning: Tamil prose body correct; all TYPEs on allow-list; male/29/Madurai content fits; Latin IDs and labs permitted; no length or script issues.

culinary_persona: Venkatramaiah Kandregula, who relishes home‑cooked rice with sambar, rasam and seasonal pulihora during Sankranti, they enjoy fresh coriander and raw mango from their garden, yet they rarely cook elaborate meals, preferring simple, hearty dishes.
persona: Venkatramaiah Kandregula, a 32‑year‑old metal puncher who thrives on routine, they find peace in sunrise walks and folk songs, they save diligently for their children while battling occasional back strain, and they prefer quiet over crowds.
cultural_background: Venkatramaiah is a member of the Telugu‑speaking Hindu community of Prakasam district in Andhra Pradesh. The community is rooted in agrarian traditions, celebrates festivals such as Sankranti, Ugadi, and Vinayaka Chavithi, and places high value on family ties, respect for elders, and collective harmony. Living in a rural setting has shaped his practical outlook, preference for routine, and appreciation for simple, work‑centered values.
linguistic_background: His mother tongue is Telugu, which he speaks fluently and uses for all everyday communication. Because he is illiterate, he cannot read or write Telugu or any other language; he relies on oral instructions, visual cues, and memorised routines to perform his work. He does not have knowledge of a second or third language.
skills_and_expertise: As a metal puncher, he is skilled in operating punching presses, setting up tools, performing routine maintenance, measuring dimensions with calipers, ensuring safety protocols, managing production schedules, and coordinating with fellow workers to keep the workflow smooth.

hobbies_and_interests: He enjoys listening to Telugu folk songs, watching popular Telugu movies during occasional evenings, tending a small vegetable garden behind his house, fishing in the nearby pond during weekends, taking solitary walks in the fields at sunrise, and quietly observing village festivals from a respectful distance.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Telugu folk songs", "Watching Telugu movies", "Home vegetable gardening", "Fishing in local pond", "Solitary sunrise walks", "Observing village festivals"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: He aspires to become a senior shift supervisor in the metal workshop, gaining supervisory experience through on‑the‑job training. He aims to secure a stable and higher income for his wife and children, ensure his children can attend school consistently, and eventually save enough to start a small metal repair business in his village after retirement.
persona_clinical_summary: A 32-year-old male metal puncher from Prakasam district, Andhra Pradesh, who is currently married and has no formal education. He speaks Telugu and works in a rural workshop, where he operates punching presses and performs routine maintenance. He is married, lives in a rural area, and prefers quiet, routine-based activities like sunrise walks.

He speaks Telugu and works in a rural workshop, where he operates punching presses and performs routine maintenance. He is married, lives in a rural area, and prefers quiet, routine-based activities like sunrise walks.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):

professional_persona: Mohan Narayanan, a retired household manager, channels his meticulous budgeting, event coordination, and culinary expertise into mentoring neighbourhood youth on financial prudence and cultural literacy, and they plan a modest home‑based pickle venture with careful notebooks.
linguistic_persona: Mohan Narayanan fluently reads and writes Urdu, converses comfortably in Tamil, and handles basic English newspaper reading and digital communication, and they seamlessly switch scripts to connect with their community.
sports_persona: Mohan Narayanan enjoys watching cricket, especially cheering for the Chennai Super Kings, plays casual chess and rummy with friends, and goes for early morning walks around his neighbourhood park to stay fit, and they prefer low‑impact activities over high‑intensity sports.
arts_persona: Mohan Narayanan recites Ghalib and Faiz at family gatherings, listens avidly to Carnatic ragas, occasionally plays the harmonium, admires classic Tamil cinema icons like Sivaji Ganesan, and they practice Urdu calligraphy as a meditative art.
travel_persona: Mohan Narayanan prefers pilgrimages to sacred Vaishnavite sites like Tirupati and Venkateswara, enjoys weekend getaways to Ooty’s tea estates, and dreams of exploring Delhi’s historic monuments to connect with his Urdu roots, and they plan trips with careful budgeting and a modest travel itinerary.
culinary_persona: Mohan Narayanan loves the aromatic tang of sambar, the crispness of idli, and the fragrant layers of biryani, enjoys savoring mango pickles and coriander chutney, but rarely cooks for himself, preferring to order street‑food chaat on busy evenings, and they occasionally experiment with homemade chutney for the pickle business.
persona: Mohan Narayanan blends Urdu poetry with Tamil traditions, budgets meticulously for his pickle dreams, and despite a reserved nature, they chat about cricket scores over evening tea.
cultural_background: Mohan grew up in the bustling urban neighbourhood of Salem, Tamil Nadu, where the streets echo with Tamil chatter, but his home resonated with Urdu poetry and North‑Indian flavours. As a Hindu family that migrated from the Uttar‑Pradesh region in the early 1970s, his cultural tapestry weaves together Tamil festivals like Pongal with Urdu literary gatherings, creating a hybrid identity that respects both the Tamil and Urdu heritage while rooted firmly in Hindu rituals.
linguistic_background: Mohan’s mother tongue is Urdu, which he reads and writes fluently thanks to early exposure at home and community gatherings. He acquired Tamil as his second language during school, achieving functional literacy and comfortable conversational skills that allow him to navigate daily life in Salem. His formal education ended at the secondary level, so his English proficiency is modest, limited to basic reading of newspapers and occasional correspondence. He is confident in both Urdu and Tamil scripts, and can switch seamlessly between languages in social settings.
skills_and_expertise: Mohan possesses practical household management skills honed over years of running his family home. He is adept at budgeting, planning daily chores, and coordinating family events. His culinary expertise spans traditional Tamil dishes such as idli, sambar, and biryani influenced by North‑Indian flavours. He maintains a modest vegetable garden, applying basic horticultural techniques. He is digitally literate, comfortable using a smartphone for WhatsApp, video calls, and online banking. His Urdu proficiency enables him to read classical poetry and translate religious verses. He also volunteers occasionally as a coordinator for temple festivals, showcasing organizational and leadership abilities.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Household Management", "Budgeting & Financial Planning", "Cooking (Tamil & North Indian Cuisine)", "Gardening", "Basic Digital Literacy", "Urdu Poetry Reading and Translation", "Temple Festival Coordination", "Basic First Aid"]
hobbies_and_interests: Mohan enjoys immersing himself in Urdu poetry, often reciting verses of Ghalib and Faiz in his quiet study. He is an avid listener of Carnatic classical music and occasionally plays the harmonium during family gatherings. Gardening provides him solace, and he tends to a small balcony garden of herbs and seasonal vegetables. He loves watching classic Tamil cinema, especially films of the golden era starring Sivaji Ganesan. Board games like chess and card games such as rummy keep his mind sharp. He also practices calligraphy, writing Urdu couplets in elegant script.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Reciting Urdu Poetry", "Listening to Carnatic Music", "Playing Harmonium", "Gardening (herbs & seasonal vegetables)", "Watching Classic Tamil Cinema", "Playing Chess", "Playing Rummy", "Practicing Calligraphy"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Although retired from formal employment, Mohan aspires to deepen his involvement in community service. He aims to mentor youth in his neighbourhood on financial prudence and cultural literacy, especially bridging Urdu and Tamil heritage. He wishes to start a small home‑based venture producing traditional pickles and chutneys, leveraging his culinary skills and local market demand. He also plans to undertake a pilgrimage to key Vaishnavite sites such as Tirupati and Venkateswara Temple, combining spiritual fulfillment with personal health goals. Additionally, he intends to improve his Tamil fluency to assist his grandchildren with schoolwork.
persona_clinical_summary: Mr. Mohan Narayanan, a 48-year-old male, is currently married and retired, residing in urban Salem, Tamil Nadu. He has a Matriculation education and speaks Urdu as his first language, with functional Tamil proficiency. His lifestyle includes meticulous budgeting and an interest in cricket, while he prefers low-impact activities like morning walks.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
